stem: A streaming service offers two monthly subscription plans: a Basic plan and a Premium plan. A household that subscribes to 3 Basic plans and 1 Premium plan pays $\$95$ per month. A household that subscribes to 1 Basic plan and 2 Premium plans pays $\$100$ per month. What is the monthly cost, in dollars, of one Basic plan?
explanation:
Let $b$ = monthly cost of the Basic plan (in dollars) and $p$ = monthly cost of the Premium plan (in dollars).

Set up the system:
- $3b + p = 95$ ... (1)
- $b + 2p = 100$ ... (2)

Multiply equation (1) by 2:
$$6b + 2p = 190 \quad \text{...(3)}$$

Subtract equation (2) from equation (3):
$$(6b + 2p) - (b + 2p) = 190 - 100$$
$$5b = 90$$
$$b = 18$$

Back-substitute into equation (2) to find $p$:
$$18 + 2p = 100$$
$$2p = 82$$
$$p = 41$$

Checking equation (1): $3(18) + 41 = 54 + 41 = 95$ ✓
Checking equation (2): $18 + 2(41) = 18 + 82 = 100$ ✓

The monthly cost of one Basic plan is $\mathbf{\$18}$.

distractorRationale.A: Makes a coefficient subtraction error when eliminating: after forming $6b + 2p = 190$, subtracts equation (2) but treats the $b$-coefficient as 0 rather than 1, writing $6b = 190 - 100 = 90$ instead of $5b = 90$, giving $b = 90 \div 6 = 15$.
distractorRationale.B: Makes a subtraction arithmetic slip: correctly forms $6b + 2p = 190$, but computes $190 - 100 = 80$ instead of 90 when subtracting, giving $5b = 80 \Rightarrow b = 16$.
distractorRationale.C: Correct answer: the Basic plan costs $\$18$ per month.
distractorRationale.D: Makes a wrong-variable error: solves the system correctly ($b = 18$, $p = 41$) but reports the cost of the Premium plan ($p = 41$) instead of the Basic plan.
